GEORGE WOLFF, ESQ., COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF ACME INTEGRATED SERVICES, INC., WAS LISTED ON THE COURTCALL CALENDAR BUT DID NOT MAKE AN APPEARANCE. THE COURT IMPOSED A $60.00 SANCTION AGAINST MR. WOLFF FOR FAILURE TO APPEAR TODAY, PAYABLE BY 03/08/24. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MOTION TO MAR-05-2024 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. COURT WILL SEND OUT AN ORDER. JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO; COURT REPORTER: DEBORAH FUQUA, CSR #12948, 415/810-7170, EMAIL: DFUQUACSR@GMAIL.COM (206) ***[IMMEDIATELY AFTER HEARING, DEPT. 206 RECEIVED AN EMAIL FROM MR. WOLFF INDICATING THAT HE WAS IN THE EMERGENCY ROOM & HAVING TROUBLE CALLING IN FROM HIS CELL PHONE. THE COURT THEREFORE AND THEREAFTER VACATED THE $60.00 SANCTIONS.]***

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint To Add Additional Surety Defendant, And To Increase Damages Claim Based On Subsequently Incurred Damages
Off Calendar Dec-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ACME INTERGRATED SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Amend Complaint To Add Additional Surety Defendant, And To Increase Damages Claim Based On Subsequently Incurred Damages.

Plaintiff Acme Integrated Services, Inc.'s motion for leave to amend its complaint is GRANTED.

Defendant Top Line Engineers, Inc. ("TLE") was the prime contractor to a public works project at the Bayshore CalTrain Station. TLE subcontracted with ACME to provide scaffolding for the site. In February 2023, ACME began filing stop job notices with the public agency owner claiming TLE failed to pay it in full. (Civ. Code 9350 et seq.) ACME brought this action on March 27, 2023, to enforce its stop job notices. In its first amended complaint ("FAC"), ACME claimed it was entitled to roughly $351,000. (FAC 16:10-14.) ACME now seeks leave to file a SAC to add a new release bond surety, Old Republic Insurance Company ("ORIC"), as a defendant and plead for additional damages totaling roughly $475,000. (MPA 2:13-15.)

Trial courts liberally permit leave to amend. (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-489.) A proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding whether to grant leave to amend. (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) "[T]he preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings, or other appropriate proceedings." (California casualty Gen Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 [overruled in part on other grounds].) Here, TLE argues leave to amend should be denied because the $475,000 claimed in ACME's third amended stop payment notice is excessive and based on falsities. (Opp. 6:13-17.) This motion, however, is not the vehicle for TLE's challenge. (See id.) For instance, Civil Code section 9400(c) provides a mechanism for the prime contractor to release funds withheld pursuant to an excessive stop payment notice. Moreover, on this record, TLE has not established that ACME is abusing the legal process.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
